nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs William FitzCorbucion was born about 1050, in Studley, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Sir William FitzCorbucion I. She died in Chillington Hall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.Chillington Hall, Wolverhampton, Staffor: イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアチリントン ハル
ja.Studley, Stratford-on-Avon, Warwickshire: イギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストゥドリー
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs William FitzCorbucionは約1050年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストゥドリーで生まれた。彼女はSir William FitzCorbucion Iとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女はイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアチリントン ハルで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs William FitzCorbucion(은)는 대략 1050년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 스투들리에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir William FitzCorbucion I(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 치링턴 홀에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs William FitzCorbucion родилась около 1050 г. в местности студли, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir William FitzCorbucion I. Она умерла в местности чиллингтон холл, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
